qa_method.name: QA-expanded structural features
qa_method.description: Sklearn classifier (LogReg / RandomForest) fed the integer QA structural packet. Two QA variants share the experiment: qa_basic = (b, e, d, a, C, F, G) algebraic packet (d=b+e, a=b+2e, C=2*d*e, F=a*b, G=d*d+e*e); qa_full = qa_basic plus modular phase (phi_b, phi_e) where phi_b = b mod (m//3) and phi_e = e mod (m//3) — the satellite divisor under qa_orbit_rules. Computed by tools.qa_ml in raw integer arithmetic.
qa_method.observer_projection: (b, e) discrete state in {1,...,24}^2 -> integer 7- or 9-tuple packet -> sklearn classifier (float-side observer). Boundary crossed once on input, once on output (predicted orbit class). No float feedback into QA layer.

datasets.name: qa_orbit_mod24_full_grid
datasets.source: tools.qa_ml.build_dataset(24) — 576 (b,e) pairs; class counts cosmos=567 satellite=8 singularity=1; ground truth qa_orbit_rules.orbit_family

calibration_provenance.learned_on: no calibration step — features are deterministic integer transforms with no learned thresholds or permutations
calibration_provenance.procedure: n/a — QA packet is a fixed algebraic feature map; only the downstream sklearn classifier is fit
calibration_provenance.domain_of_origin: self-contained (mod-24 full grid), no inheritance from another domain

diffs: first QA-ML feature benchmark; no prior framework being ported

source_mapping.primary_source: 0 errors against simulation for mod-9 and mod-24
source_mapping.mapping_rationale: The QA structural packet (b, e, d, a, C, F, G) carries the algebraic relationships that determine orbit class (singularity at (m,m); satellite when (m//3) divides both b and e). The benchmark tests whether exposing these derived integer features improves a generic ML classifier's sample efficiency vs. the raw (b, e) input. Ground-truth labels come from qa_orbit_rules.orbit_family, the canonical algebraic classifier.

ablation.destroyed_structure: Cycle the input feature matrix between raw (b, e), qa = (b, e, d, a, C, F, G), and qa_full = (b, e, d, a, C, F, G, phi_b, phi_e) under identical seed/split/model. The raw -> qa -> qa_full progression isolates the contribution of QA algebraic columns and then the modular-phase columns.
ablation.expected_direction: qa_full should dominate at small n_train because phi_b, phi_e expose the satellite divisor directly; qa should provide a smaller lift over raw via the polynomial structure (d, a, C, F, G); raw should lag until n_train is large enough for the model to recover the modular AND-predicate from coordinate splits.
